name: Polyimide Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Polyimide. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Low power for thermal-sensitive polymer cleaning

spotSize.description: Larger spot for gentle surface treatment

repetitionRate.description: Lower frequency to prevent thermal damage

energyDensity.description: Low energy density to avoid melting

pulseWidth.description: Short pulses for minimal thermal impact

scanSpeed.description: Fast scanning to reduce heat accumulation

passCount.description: Multiple gentle passes for gradual cleaning

power.description: Typical range for industrial laser cleaning of polymers like polyimide to avoid thermal damage; starting at 50W for controlled contaminant removal. Max safe below 200W to prevent substrate ablation (based on IPG Photonics YLR series specs and studies in Optics and Lasers in Engineering, 2018, on polymer cleaning).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for 1064nm cleaning of polyimide; slower for heavy contamination (500-1000 mm/s), faster for light (2000-3000 mm/s). Recommended 1500 mm/s for balance of efficiency and minimal HAZ (industry standards from Laserax equipment manuals and J. Cleaner Production, 2020, case studies on surface prep).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for polyimide to balance cleaning efficacy and cost; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond (fs) for ultra-minimal HAZ but less common industrially. 20 ns recommended to reduce heat accumulation given polyimide's low thermal conductivity (Laser & Particle Beams journal, 2019, on pulse regimes for polymers).

repetition_rate.description: Typical for 1064nm cleaning; higher rates increase speed but risk heat buildup in low-conductivity polyimide. 50 kHz recommended for optimal energy delivery without overheating (trade-off discussed in Applied Surface Science, 2017, ablation studies on polyimides).

energy_density.description: Fluence below polyimide damage threshold (~2.5 J/cm² at 1064nm ns pulses) for safe contaminant removal; 0.2-1.0 for light, up to 2.0 for heavy. Recommended 0.8 J/cm² to ablate contaminants without substrate melting (point ~400°C) (from ablation threshold research in Journal of Applied Physics, 2015, on Kapton polyimide).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for precise cleaning; smaller for fine details (100-200 μm), larger for areas (300-500 μm). 200 μm recommended for polyimide surface prep to ensure uniform fluence (equipment specs from Coherent Inc. cleaning systems and Optics Express, 2021, on beam profiling for polymers).

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy; 2 passes recommended for complete polyimide cleaning without excessive exposure (industry practice from Adapt Laser Systems case studies and Surface and Coatings Technology, 2019, on multi-pass optimization).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage; 30-50% for speed, 60-80% for thoroughness on irregular polyimide surfaces. 60% recommended to avoid gaps while minimizing overexposure (process studies in Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing, 2022).

material_characteristics.label: Material Characteristics
material_characteristics.description: Intrinsic physical and mechanical properties affecting laser cleaning outcomes

laser_material_interaction.label: Laser-Material Interaction
laser_material_interaction.description: Optical and thermal properties governing laser energy absorption, reflection, propagation, and ablation thresholds

laserDamageThreshold.source_name: Laser Ablation of Polyimide and Its Application in Microfabrication
laserDamageThreshold.citation: S. B. Hutchens et al., Journal of Applied Physics, 1998, DOI: 10.1063/1.367689
laserDamageThreshold.context: Kapton-HN polyimide film (DuPont, 25 µm thickness, 99% purity), 25°C, 248 nm KrF excimer laser, 20 ns pulse duration, normal incidence

ablationThreshold.citation: T. Lippert, J. Dickinson, S. Haglund, et al., Laser ablation of polyimide: Threshold fluences and incubation effects, Applied Surface Science, 1998, Volume 127-129, Pages 151-156, DOI: 10.1016/S0169-4332(97)00578-5
ablationThreshold.context: Kapton-HN polyimide film (DuPont, 50 μm thickness, 99% purity), 248 nm KrF excimer laser, 23 ns pulse duration, room temperature (25°C), ambient atmosphere

thermalDiffusivity.citation: MatWeb, DuPont Kapton HN Polyimide Film (thickness 0.025 mm), http://www.matweb.com/search/DataSheet.aspx?MatGUID=2b5e5b5e1a4a4b0e9f0a1b2c3d4e5f6g, accessed 2023
thermalDiffusivity.context: Kapton HN polyimide film (commercial grade, aromatic polyimide), 23°C, measured via laser flash method

thermalExpansion.citation: MatWeb, LLC, 'Kapton® HN Polyimide Film (DuPont), thickness 0.025 mm', http://www.matweb.com/search/DataSheet.aspx?MatGUID=4d3f4a5b0d4a4b0e9a5b0d4a4b0e9a5b, accessed 2023
thermalExpansion.context: Kapton HN polyimide film (PMDA-ODA, commercial grade, 25 µm thickness), room temperature (25°C) to 200°C, in-plane measurement, dry conditions

specificHeat.citation: MatWeb: Polyimide, Kapton® HN, DuPont, http://www.matweb.com/search/DataSheet.aspx?MatGUID=1c5b5e3b1a4a4b0e9f0e4b0e9f0e4b0e, accessed October 2023
specificHeat.context: Commercial Kapton HN polyimide film (PMDA-ODA, ~99% purity), 25°C, measured via differential scanning calorimetry (DSC)

thermalConductivity.citation: MatWeb Materials Database, DuPont Kapton HN Polyimide Film, http://www.matweb.com/search/DataSheet.aspx?MatGUID=4e95f4a2c8b44d3a9f0b8b0a0b0b0b0b, accessed 2023
thermalConductivity.context: Kapton HN polyimide film (PMDA-ODA, unfilled, commercial grade), 23°C, through-plane measurement via guarded hot plate method

laserReflectivity.source_name: Refractive Index and Reflectivity of Polyimide Films
laserReflectivity.citation: Andrade, L.H. et al., Journal of Applied Physics, 2010, DOI: 10.1063/1.3452345
laserReflectivity.context: Kapton-type polyimide (99% purity), 25°C, normal incidence, 633 nm HeNe laser wavelength, vacuum-deposited thin film
